title: EPF Anchor File
description: Schema for _epf.yaml — the anchor file that identifies a valid EPF instance directory and configures its deployment mode.
$comment: The _epf.yaml file is the root marker for any EPF instance. Its presence (with epf_anchor: true) is how tools discover and validate EPF directories. The file also carries deployment configuration that tells epf-cli how to push changes to consumer repos. Fields instance_id and created_at are write-once — generated at init, never modified. The deployment section is optional but recommended for multi-repo setups.

epf_anchor.description: Must be true. Marks this file as a valid EPF anchor.

instance_id.description: Unique identifier for this EPF instance. Generated once at init, never changed.

created_at.description: When this EPF instance was initialized.

epf_version.description: EPF framework version this instance uses (e.g., '2.0.0').

product_name.description: Name of the product this instance represents.

structure.description: Directory structure configuration for the instance.

type.description: Directory structure type. 'phased' = READY/FIRE/AIM directories, 'flat' = legacy single-level layout.

deployment.description: How this EPF instance is deployed and used across repositories.

mode.description: Deployment mode. 'integrated': EPF lives inside a product repo (e.g., docs/EPF/). 'submodule': EPF is its own repo, mounted as a git submodule in consumer repos. 'standalone': EPF repo not connected to any product repo.

consumers.description: Repos that consume this EPF instance as a git submodule. Only relevant when mode is 'submodule'.

path.description: Path where the submodule is mounted in the consumer repo (e.g., 'docs/strategy').

branch.description: Branch in the consumer repo where submodule updates are pushed.

auto_update.description: Whether epf-cli push should automatically update this consumer's submodule pointer.

source.description: Source repo info. Only relevant when mode is 'integrated'.

path.description: Path within the repo where the EPF instance lives (e.g., 'docs/EPF/_instances/my-product').
